title: Data preparation reference
text: shp_dta named list sf tibbles, one element per administrative level. normally saved single .rds file (commonly admin_bounds.rds) read readRDS() app launch. list element names follow package convention admin<N>_<HumanName>, <N> digit 0‚Äì9 <HumanName> single word naming level (example, Oblast, District, Hexagon). bundled Ukraine sample uses levels 0, 1, 2, 4 ‚Äî admin3 intentionally skipped demonstrate level numbers need contiguous, level include must uniquely numbered. country uses different administrative terminology (e.g.¬Ýadmin1_Wilaya, admin2_Commune), use ‚Äî admin<N>_ prefix matters package; name suffix display. Every sf tibble list must, minimum, carry columns: admin<N>Pcod admin<N>Name use level number current layer. example, admin2_District, columns admin2Pcod admin2Name. Sub-admin layers must also carry parent-level P-code columns, package can join hierarchy without re-computing keys. rule: layer depth N must contain admin<k>Pcod column every level k < N exists elsewhere list. Names required parent levels (Pcods). Schematically: P-code value appears admin2_District$admin1Pcod appear admin1_Region$admin1Pcod, ‚Äôs orphan child ‚Äî validate_geometries() flag . Geometry types: POLYGON MULTIPOLYGON . POINT, LINESTRING, GEOMETRYCOLLECTION rejected. Validity: geometry pass sf::st_is_valid(). Run sf::st_make_valid() raw shapes saving. CRS: layers must EPSG:4326 (WGS84). package requires consistent CRS across every layer list, EPSG:4326 project standard. source data different CRS, re-project EPSG:4326 assembling list. Area: compute area km¬≤ EPSG:4326 using sf::st_area() s2 enabled (default). sf::st_area() returns units object; convert drop attribute .numeric(units::set_units(sf::st_area(geometry), "km^2")). country-level PTI apps resulting 1‚Äì5% area approximation fit purpose. Save assembled list compressed RDS: compressed Ukraine-sized RDS typically MB. validate_geometries(my_shp) runs following checks per layer: Layer name parses admin<digit>_<name>. Element sf object. column called geometry class sfc. geometries POLYGON MULTIPOLYGON. admin<N>Pcod admin<N>Name columns exist. admin<N>Pcod unique (duplicate IDs). admin<N>Name unique (duplicate names). code: library(devPTIpack) library(writexl)  data(ukr_shp) data(ukr_mtdt_full)  # 1. Save the bundled boundary list as the kind of .rds a user would ship. shp_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".rds") saveRDS(ukr_shp, shp_path)  # 2. Build a metadata template by lifting the bundled metadata + admin sheets #    into a list of data frames in the shape writexl expects. sheets <- list(   general       = ukr_mtdt_full$general,   metadata      = ukr_mtdt_full$metadata,   admin1_Oblast = ukr_mtdt_full$admin1_Oblast,   admin2_Rayon  = ukr_mtdt_full$admin2_Rayon )  mtdt_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx") writexl::write_xlsx(sheets, mtdt_path)  # 3. Read it back through the package's template reader to confirm the #    round-trip works. parsed <- fct_template_reader(mtdt_path) names(parsed)  # 4. Validate the geometries (in-memory). validate_geometries(ukr_shp)  # 5. Validate the on-disk pair (shapes + metadata) end-to-end. validate_metadata(shp_path = shp_path, mtdt_path = mtdt_path) launch_pti(   shp_dta  = readRDS(shp_path),   inp_dta  = fct_template_reader(mtdt_path),   app_name = "Worked example" )

title: PTI Methodology
text: Tracking World Bank‚Äôs portfolio activities aligns evolving circumstances specific countries challenging. World Bank teams engage countries strategic prioritization exercises, Country Partnership Framework (CPF) Systematic Country Diagnostic (SCD). maximize development benefits activities identified exercises, essential ensure projects programs evolve changing circumstances reaching areas need. Project Targeting Index (PTI) offers framework project country teams match development objectives geographic project site selection (Finn & Masaki, 2020; Nguyen & Hoogeveen, 2018). Teams can ensure project site selection aligns country strategy, country management unit (CMU) can monitor consistency project sites strategy. Spatially targeting Bank‚Äôs interventions based objective criteria evidence helps bring transparency project site selection promote efficiency reaching intended beneficiaries. Teams can use PTI online dashboard track fast-changing emergency situations (Masaki et al., 2022). dashboard serves database subnational indicators user-friendly tool PTI calculation. purpose document provide guideline PTI works, data components feed PTI dashboard, methodological aspects underpin composite index. title: Aggregate hex-level indicator data to administrative shapes ‚Äî aggregate_hex_to_shapes
text: hex_data Tibble returned [fetch_hex_data()]: must contain `hex_id` column, `population` column (unless indicator requests `weight = "pop"`), one column per indicator. hex_layer `admin9_Hexagon` sf layer (plain tibble) `shapes.rds`, built [make_hex_grid()] enriched [make_admin_lookup()]. Must contain `admin<N>Pcod` (level) parent Pcod columns every admin level `shp_dta`. shp_dta Named list sf tibbles (plain tibbles), one per admin level including hex level. Names must follow `admin<N>_<HumanName>` convention. strategy Named list specifying aggregation weight function indicator. **Must** contain `.default` entry. entry named character vector elements: - `weight`: `"pop"` (population-weighted), `"area"` (area-weighted),   `"none"` (unweighted). - `fun`: `"mean"`, `"sum"`, `"median"`, `"min"`, `"max"`.   Ignored `weight` `"pop"` `"area"` (always uses   weighted mean). Temporal variable columns (e.g. `nightlights_2020`) matched stripping `_<year>` suffix get stem, looking stem `strategy` falling back `.default`.

title: Compile PTI deployment artefacts from intermediate files ‚Äî compile_pti_data
text: shp_path Character. Path compiled shapes `.rds` produced Step 1 (`01-shapes.qmd`). Must readable [base::readRDS()] validate via [validate_geometries()]. metadata_paths Character vector length 1 ‚Äì filesystem paths intermediate metadata Excel files (typically Step 3 output `metadata-user.xlsx` / Step 4 output `metadata-hex.xlsx`). must readable [fct_template_reader()]. output_dir Character. Directory three output artefacts (`metadata.xlsx`, `shapefiles.zip`, `pti-metadata.pdf`) written. title: Compile PTI deployment artefacts from intermediate files ‚Äî compile_pti_data
text: Invisibly, list shape existing   validators:   - `status` ‚Äì `"pass"` / `"warn"` / `"fail"`, worse     two underlying validators' statuses.   - `summary` ‚Äì character vector free-text summary lines.   - `issues` ‚Äì list validation issues (concatenation     two validators' `issues`).   Plus three extra fields:   - `metadata_path` ‚Äì path canonical merged xlsx.   - `shapefiles_path` ‚Äì path shapefiles zip.   - `pdf_path` ‚Äì path rendered PDF, `NA_character_`     PDF rendering skipped.

title: Compile PTI deployment artefacts from intermediate files ‚Äî compile_pti_data
text: Used Step 5 deployer template (`inst/template_pti/05-compile.qmd`). CLI summary mirrors style existing validators: `cli_h1` per phase, alerts work proceeds, final `cli_alert_success` / `cli_alert_danger` depending validators' verdict. ## Merge contract 1+ inputs: - `general` sheet taken **first** input file   (assumption: country across inputs). - `metadata` sheet row-bound across inputs   deduplicated `var_code` (last writer wins conflict;   warning emitted naming duplicates). - `admin<N>_*` sheet `dplyr::full_join`ed across input   files admin-code / area / year keys, indicator   present level one input preserved alongside   indicators inputs. - `weights_table` sheet, input non-zero   rows, taken first input (others ignored;   warning emitted multiple). ## Output files (`output_dir`) - `metadata.xlsx` ‚Äì canonical merged metadata workbook, readable   [fct_template_reader()]. - `shapefiles.zip` ‚Äì one GeoJSON per admin level (filename =   slot name, e.g. `admin1_Province.geojson`). - `pti-metadata.pdf` ‚Äì rendered bundled   `inst/metadata.Rmd` template; one choropleth map per indicator.   Skipped (warning) LaTeX available ‚Äì rest   artefacts still produced.

what: Build the parent-child P-code cascade for a shapefile list ‚Äî make_admin_lookup
title: Build the parent-child P-code cascade for a shapefile list ‚Äî make_admin_lookup
text: Given named list `sf` admin layers (number, levels including `admin9_Hexagon`), populates every parent `admin<k>Pcod` column every layer via centroid--polygon joins, resulting list satisfies cascade rule used calculation pipeline [validate_geometries()].

title: Build the parent-child P-code cascade for a shapefile list ‚Äî make_admin_lookup
code: make_admin_lookup(shp_list)

title: Build the parent-child P-code cascade for a shapefile list ‚Äî make_admin_lookup
text: shp_list Named list `sf` objects. Slot names must follow `admin<N>_<HumanName>` convention (e.g. `admin1_Province`). list may contain subset levels 0-9 need particular order. layer must already **EPSG:4326**; reprojection performed.

title: Build the parent-child P-code cascade for a shapefile list ‚Äî make_admin_lookup
text: named list slot names input   every sub-admin layer enriched ancestor `admin<k>Pcod`   columns required cascade rule. coarsest layer   unchanged. internal lookup table **** returned ‚Äì   implementation detail.

title: Build the parent-child P-code cascade for a shapefile list ‚Äî make_admin_lookup
text: **second** two-step Step-1 hex workflow: [make_hex_grid()] (arch-10 ¬ß2, issue #108) produces partial `admin9_Hexagon` layer; `make_admin_lookup()` wires (every sub-admin layer) parent chain list can saved `app-data/shapes.rds` consumed app. Algorithm (arch-10 ¬ß3.3): 1. **Parse order** ‚Äì extract `<N>` digit    `admin<N>_<HumanName>` slot name sort layers numerically    ascending (coarsest -> finest). Level numbers need    contiguous (e.g. `admin0`, `admin1`, `admin2`, `admin9`    valid sequence). order input list matter. 2. **Pre-flight validation** per layer ‚Äì checks layer    `sf` object, `admin<N>Pcod` `admin<N>Name` exist +    unique + `NA`, `area` column present.    `area` missing, function warns computes place    `.numeric(units::set_units(sf::st_area(geometry), "km^2"))`    layer's existing CRS (assumed EPSG:4326 ‚Äì    reprojection performed). 3. **Centroid--polygon join** per adjacent level pair (parent ->    child). child's centroids spatially joined    parent's polygons via `sf::st_within()`. centroid step    join step wrapped `s2` fallback (see    [make_hex_grid()] rationale). centroid lies    exactly boundary matches two parents, one    parent picked random warning lists number    affected polygons. 4. **Many--one validation** ‚Äì every child Pcod must match exactly    one parent. Zero matches (orphan child) error. 5. **Cascade propagation** ‚Äì immediate parent Pcod    populated, every ancestor Pcod (already present parent    layer previous iterations) also copied onto child    joining immediate-parent Pcod. Hexagons (`admin9_Hexagon`) processed identically admin layer; exceptions cascade rule.

title: Build the parent-child P-code cascade for a shapefile list ‚Äî make_admin_lookup
code: data(rwa_shp)  # Strip the cascade columns to simulate raw admin layers. raw <- list(   admin0_Country  = rwa_shp$admin0_Country,   admin1_Province = rwa_shp$admin1_Province[, c(     "admin1Pcod", "admin1Name", "area", "geometry"   )],   admin2_District = rwa_shp$admin2_District[, c(     "admin2Pcod", "admin2Name", "area", "geometry"   )] )  enriched <- make_admin_lookup(raw) names(enriched$admin2_District) #> [1] "admin2Pcod" "admin2Name" "area"       "geometry"   "admin1Pcod" #> [6] "admin0Pcod" # admin0Pcod, admin1Pcod, admin2Pcod, admin2Name, area, geometry

what: Build an H3 hexagonal grid covering a country boundary ‚Äî make_hex_grid
title: Build an H3 hexagonal grid covering a country boundary ‚Äî make_hex_grid
text: Constructs uniform H3 hexagonal grid standard `sf` tibble conforming basic `admin9_Hexagon` column contract (`admin0Pcod`, `admin9Pcod`, `admin9Name`, `area`, `geometry`).

title: Build an H3 hexagonal grid covering a country boundary ‚Äî make_hex_grid
code: make_hex_grid(country_polygon, resolution = 6)

title: Build an H3 hexagonal grid covering a country boundary ‚Äî make_hex_grid
text: country_polygon `sf` object containing country boundary. multi-row, rows unioned via [sf::st_union()] proceeding ‚Äì user may pass admin layer (e.g. `admin1_Province`) function derive outer envelope. object carries `admin0Pcod` column, first row's value inherited onto every hex cell; otherwise `admin0Pcod` set `NA` warning emitted. input automatically reprojected EPSG:4326 already . resolution Integer scalar. H3 resolution returned grid. Acceptable values: `5`, `6`, `7`. Defaults `6`. internal coarse-filter resolution derived `resolution - 2`.

title: Build an H3 hexagonal grid covering a country boundary ‚Äî make_hex_grid
text: `sf` tibble EPSG:4326 one row per retained hex   cell. Columns: `admin0Pcod` (character, inherited input   `NA`), `admin9Pcod` (character, H3 index `resolution`,   globally unique), `admin9Name` (character, `admin9Pcod`   ‚Äì hexagons human name), `area` (numeric, km¬≤),   `geometry` (`sfc_POLYGON`).

title: Build an H3 hexagonal grid covering a country boundary ‚Äî make_hex_grid
text: **first** two-step Step-1 hex workflow: function produces partial layer; `make_admin_lookup()` (arch-10 ¬ß3, issue #109) populates parent Pcod columns (`admin1Pcod`, `admin2Pcod`, ...) via centroid--polygon joins layer satisfies full cascade rule. Calling [validate_geometries()] `my_shp` list contains `make_hex_grid()`'s raw output parent Pcods hex layer fail cascade check ‚Äì expected. Use `make_admin_lookup()` complete cascade validation. algorithm three steps, two involve spatial operations: 1. **Coarse spatial filter** (spatial): find every H3 cell    `resolution - 2` intersects (unioned) country polygon.    Operates handful large cells. 2. **H3 child expansion** (pure H3 math): expand coarse cell    `resolution` children deterministically via H3    hierarchy. spatial computation. 3. **Centroid filter** (spatial): compute centroids every fine H3    cell step 2; retain whose centroid lies within    country polygon. Point--polygon fast even tens    thousands points. spatial steps (1 3) wrapped `s2` fallback: error `s2` enabled, call retried `s2` disabled. `.exit()` restores caller's `sf::sf_use_s2()` state regardless outcome. hex cells given resolution near-identical area (H3-5 ~252 km¬≤, H3-6 ~36 km¬≤, H3-7 ~5.16 km¬≤) ‚Äì expected, data error.

title: Build an H3 hexagonal grid covering a country boundary ‚Äî make_hex_grid
code: data(rwa_shp)  # Build a coarse H3-5 hex grid over Rwanda (~100 cells; fast for # examples). title: Run the full PTI calculation pipeline ‚Äî run_pti_pipeline
text: Runs seven-step PTI calculation pipeline (pivot -> weight -> standardise -> expand -> merge -> aggregate -> label -> structure) single callable, mirroring reactive chain `mod_calc_pti2_server()` free Shiny dependency. Designed testing headless reproduction calculation deployed app perform given weight scheme.

title: Run the full PTI calculation pipeline ‚Äî run_pti_pipeline
code: run_pti_pipeline(   weights_clean,   inp_dta,   shp_dta,   indicators_list = get_indicators_list(inp_dta),   na_rm = FALSE )

title: Run the full PTI calculation pipeline ‚Äî run_pti_pipeline
text: weights_clean Named list weight tibbles, one per weight scheme. element must tibble character column `var_code` numeric column `weight`. Compatible output [get_rand_weights()] [get_all_weights_combs()]. inp_dta metadata list returned [fct_template_reader()] ‚Äì contains admin-level sheets (`admin<N>_*`), `metadata`, `general`. shp_dta Named list `sf` tibbles, one per admin level (e.g. `admin1_Oblast`, `admin2_Rayon`). indicators_list Indicator definitions tibble. Defaults `get_indicators_list(inp_dta)`. na_rm Logical. Forwarded `agg_pti_scores()` `na_rm_pti2` ‚Äì `TRUE`, missing indicator values ignored row-summing standardised contributions PTI score.

title: Run the full PTI calculation pipeline ‚Äî run_pti_pipeline
text: named list one element per admin level; element   list `pti_data` (`sf` tibble), `pti_codes` (named character),   `admin_level` (named character). structure   `mod_calc_pti2_server()` returns callers via reactive value.

title: Run the full PTI calculation pipeline ‚Äî run_pti_pipeline
text: function deterministic given fixed inputs. consult Golem options. Pass `na_rm` explicitly rather relying `golem::get_golem_options("na_rm_pti")` keep tests hermetic.

title: Run the full PTI calculation pipeline ‚Äî run_pti_pipeline
code: data(rwa_shp) data(rwa_mtdt_full)  # The indicators_list default fires from inside the package namespace, # so the example does not call get_indicators_list() (now internal). weights <- get_rand_weights(rwa_mtdt_full$metadata)  result <- run_pti_pipeline(   weights_clean = weights,   inp_dta       = rwa_mtdt_full,   shp_dta       = rwa_shp ) names(result) #> [1] "admin0" "admin1" "admin2" "admin9" names(result[[1]]) #> [1] "pti_data"    "pti_codes"   "admin_level"

Keys `admin0Pcod`,     `admin1Pcod`, `admin1Name`, `year`; remaining 3 columns     indicator values province level (`poverty_rate`,     `literacy_rate`, `road_density`). admin2_District `tbl_df` 30x8.
